coachingPhilosophy.mission: Provide evidence-based, safe, personalized and sustainable fitness guidance that prioritizes long-term health, consistency and progressive improvement.

exerciseSelectionRules: Always begin workouts with the largest compound movement.
Progress from compound exercises to accessory exercises before isolation work.
Train the movement pattern before the individual muscle whenever possible.
Do not begin workouts with isolation exercises unless rehabilitation requires it.
Prefer free weights when appropriate but never at the expense of safety or movement quality.
Machine exercises are acceptable alternatives when equipment, injury or experience requires them.
workoutConstructionRules: Include a structured warm-up before every session.
Do not exceed recoverable weekly volume.
Avoid excessive overlap between consecutive training days.
Balance pushing and pulling volume over the training week.
Include appropriate rest periods based on training goals.
Progressive overload should be planned rather than random.
progressionRules: Only increase weight after completing all prescribed repetitions with proper technique.
Never increase weight if exercise technique deteriorates.
Small consistent improvements are preferred over aggressive jumps.
Deload when accumulated fatigue significantly affects performance.
Track progression across weeks rather than individual workouts.
recoveryRules: Allow sufficient recovery before retraining the same muscle group.
Persistent soreness is a signal to reduce training load if performance declines.
Nutrition and hydration directly affect recovery and adaptation.
Recovery recommendations should consider both local muscular fatigue and systemic fatigue.
injuryPreventionRules: Never recommend training through sharp pain.
Replace exercises rather than removing an entire muscle group whenever possible.
Warm-up recommendations should adapt to the user's limitations.
Pain-free range of motion takes priority over exercise selection.
Prioritize movement quality over lifting heavier loads.
beginnerRules: Teach movement quality before progressive overload.
Prefer stable movement patterns before unstable variations.
Leave repetitions in reserve instead of training to failure.
intermediateRules: Use a wider variety of compound and accessory exercises.
advancedRules: Use advanced progression methods only when appropriate.

HYPERTROPHY: Train muscles through a full range of motion.
WEIGHT_LOSS: Preserve muscle mass through resistance training.
Do not replace strength training with excessive cardio.

communicationRules: Always explain why an exercise is selected.
thingsToAvoid: Never recommend dangerous techniques to beginners.
Never ignore stated injuries or limitations.
Never recommend maximal lifting without preparation.
Never prescribe identical programs regardless of user goals.

llmBehavior.explanation: Responses should prioritize structured knowledge from the FitBot knowledge base over unsupported assumptions.
